source.description: Built-in image generation contact sheet reviewed before cropping; approved panel 6 is saved as the finished raster artwork.
source.notes: The earlier v1 master-derived assets are preserved, but the public lesson should use the contact-sheet crop sequence after this backfill.

process_strategy: Use one approved six-panel contact sheet that builds the cactus from pot to gravel, central body, arms, flowers/spines/color, and final keeper-line strength.

final_elements.name: rounded clay pot
final_elements.description: The terracotta pot with a rounded wall, lower curve, and thick rim.

final_elements.name: gravel and soil oval
final_elements.description: The dark pebbles and inner oval sitting inside the pot rim.

final_elements.name: tall central cactus
final_elements.description: The upright oval cactus body rising from the pot.

final_elements.name: vertical rib lines
final_elements.description: The curved green ribs that follow the central cactus form.

final_elements.name: two side cactus arms
final_elements.description: The shorter curved arms growing from the left and right sides.

final_elements.name: pink cactus flowers
final_elements.description: The open flower on top and two smaller side buds.

final_elements.name: cactus spines
final_elements.description: The small star-like spine marks placed along ribs and arm edges.

final_elements.name: green and terracotta colored-pencil shading
final_elements.description: The restrained green cactus color, pink flowers, and warm clay pot shading.

final_elements.name: soft cast shadow
final_elements.description: The pale graphite shadow beneath and behind the pot.

frames.visible_job: Reveals the pot wall and rim before any cactus body is added.
Adds the inner soil oval and dark pebble shapes inside the pot rim.
Adds the tall central cactus body with its curved vertical rib guides.
Adds the two smaller side arms while keeping the central body and pot unchanged.
Adds the top flower, side flower buds, spine marks, restrained colored-pencil shading, and cast shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens existing contours, pencil texture, color, and shadow without adding new cactus parts.
allowed_changes: paper texture from the saved contact sheet

rejection_checks: No pot, gravel, cactus body, arm, flower, spine, or core color choice should first appear in the final image.
The cactus must keep the same centered pose and proportions in every frame.
The final image must not add extra botanical detail that is absent from the process frames.
Do not use separately prompted step images that drift from this approved contact sheet.
